name: Autolycus
description: Autolycus, in Greek mythology, was a son of Hermes and Chione, renowned for his cunning and thievery. Blessed by his father with the ability to change the appearance of anything he stole, and even of himself, Autolycus became the most skilled and elusive thief of his time. He was a master of deception, capable of outwitting even the most vigilant guards and concealing his crimes with ease. His exploits are legendary, and he is often depicted as a mischievous trickster rather than a purely malevolent figure. Despite his criminal tendencies, Autolycus possessed a certain charm and wit that made him a memorable character in Greek lore. He is perhaps best known as the grandfather of Odysseus, inheriting some of his cunning and resourcefulness. His role in the story of Odysseus's scar is significant, as it was Autolycus who named the infant Odysseus. Autolycus's skills extended beyond thievery; he was also a skilled wrestler and athlete, further showcasing his multifaceted nature. His legacy is one of cunning, deception, and a touch of roguish charm, solidifying his place as a prominent figure in Greek mythology, particularly in stories involving trickery and clever schemes. He is a reminder that not all heroes are noble and virtuous, and that sometimes, a little bit of cunning can go a long way.
subtitle: The Master Thief and Trickster
achievements: Changing the appearance of stolen goods

keyMyths.title: The Stolen Cattle of Sisyphus
keyMyths.description: Autolycus stole cattle from Sisyphus, but Sisyphus suspected him. Sisyphus marked his cattle to identify them, revealing Autolycus's theft.

keyMyths.title: The Naming of Odysseus
keyMyths.description: Autolycus visited his daughter Anticlea after Odysseus's birth. He named the child Odysseus, meaning 'son of pain' or 'son of suffering'.

keyMyths.title: Teaching Heracles
keyMyths.description: Autolycus is sometimes credited with teaching Heracles the art of wrestling. This highlights Autolycus's diverse skills beyond thievery.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Provides key information about Autolycus's role in Odysseus's life and naming.
sources.text: Provides key information about Autolycus's role in Odysseus's life and naming.

sources.author: Hyginus
sources.description: Details Autolycus's thievery, including the story of the stolen cattle from Sisyphus.
